blocks.content: Siena threw down the evaluation sheet and apologized to Alpheus.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Headmaster. You came all this way to observe, and this is what you had to see.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Hohoho. It was a challenging task, indeed. Isn't that what supernatural spiritual science is all about? Don't trouble yourself over it.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I'll make sure to give them a stern warning.
Alpheus waved his hand dismissively and headed for the exit.
But just before stepping out, he turned back and glanced at the empty stage.
The corners of his lips curled up beneath his beard.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: 'If this were truly the end, they wouldn't have started in the first place. Isn't that right, Shirone?'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We did it! Phase one is complete!
Despite the audience's reaction, Shirone and his group hurried back to their research lab.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Do you think it worked?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Yeah. I could tell just by their expressions.
They had anticipated the disappointment—after all, the grander the expectations, the more underwhelming the reveal would seem.
But Shirone had counted on this. The more dismissive the audience was, the more susceptible they'd be to psychological manipulation.
Mass hypnosis.
The key was the midnight bell. Once the idea was planted, it didn't matter how absurd it seemed.
The keyword would trigger their imaginations and lead them straight into terror.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, it's all up to you now. If you succeed, we win.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Got it. I'll meditate here for a while.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You have to succeed! We're counting on you!
They had given it their all for the past month. Now, the final responsibility fell on Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I need to focus. Give me some space.
Shirone stopped Neid from patting his shoulder and sat down in a secluded spot.
Six hours remained. He had to elevate his mental state to its peak within that time.
Shirone lowered his head and slowly began counting.
Neid and Iruki tiptoed away, careful not to make a sound.
The success of this project hinged on whether Shirone could sustain photon emission for over ten minutes.
But the others still had work to do.
Iruki had to set up the remote control for the holograms, while Neid fine-tuned the core that would channel Shirone's energy.
As everyone busied themselves with their tasks, night fell, and midnight approached.
In the pitch-black darkness, Shirone's eyes snapped open.
After five hours of meditation, his mind was as sharp as a honed blade.
Soon, the door creaked open, and Neid's voice whispered, 'Shirone, it's time.'
Shirone stood up smoothly, as if it were nothing, and followed Neid out like a gladiator entering the Colosseum.
The hologram core was installed in the sewers beneath the central park.
Dozens of fiber-optic cables stretched upward from the massive device, branching into hundreds more through multi-adapters.
A staggering 300 hologram units had been deployed for this operation.
Shirone was amazed by Neid's production capabilities, but the pressure weighed heavily on him.
Though he had managed to sustain photon emission for ten minutes during practice, this was the real deal.
No one could predict what might go wrong.
With ten minutes left until midnight, Shirone approached a machine ten times larger than the prototype.
Neid handed him the input terminal with a solemn expression.
Shirone gripped the crystal orb with both hands.
Iruki stood by with a stopwatch, ready to time it perfectly.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Ready? It's time.
Shirone took a deep breath and nodded.
Iruki raised his hand, watching the clock intently.
Simultaneously, Shirone's counting accelerated explosively.
Light erupted from his hands with unprecedented intensity.
His friends marveled at how much his photon output had improved in just a month.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: More! Stronger!
The light grew until it threatened to consume Shirone.
Neid rushed to the core device to check the transmission efficiency.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Seventeen percent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What?!
It was far lower than expected.
Where had they gone wrong?
But there was no time to analyze.
Failure now meant total defeat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The range is too wide. It's impossible like this.
Shirone had sensed it immediately.
The moment he began emitting photons, it felt like pouring water into a bottomless pit—an endless void draining his energy.
The fiber-optic transmission efficiency was only 18%.
The parallel connections had weakened the signal further.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I need to focus. Give me some space.
Shirone kept pushing, but no matter how hard he tried, the efficiency wouldn't rise beyond 17%.
Iruki realized it was hopeless.
Activating the holograms now would only shatter them into fragments.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shirone, that's enough. We did our best. Let's stop here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: After coming this far…
Shirone closed his eyes, steeling his resolve.
He hadn't given everything yet.
The light in his hands swelled, engulfing his entire body.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: W-wait… are you serious?! Immortal Function.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Na… Neid…

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Yes, Shirone! Let's stop! Let's end it here!
The corners of Shirone's lips curled slightly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm… not afraid anymore.
The moment Shirone closed his eyes again, a brilliant light flooded the tunnel.
His mental energy began expanding infinitely.
There was no stopping it now.
Neid and Iruki stumbled back, their faces pale.
All they could do was pray for Shirone's safe return.
In the infinite realm, Shirone felt a familiar sensation.
The feeling that everything constituting his existence was meaningless.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But this time, he refused to forget himself.
I won't look away. I'll face it head-on.
A monstrous entity lashed its tongue, trying to devour him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll overcome this. I'll conquer it.
Grrr…!
His mind collided with the barrier of the Spirit Zone, sending a searing pain through his skull.
He had caught it.
Gritting his teeth, Shirone endured.
The overflowing energy surged into photon emission.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shirone! Are you okay? Say something!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Check the output first!
At Shirone's shout, Neid turned to the core.
Transmission efficiency: 22%.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: It's rising, it's rising! Shirone!
Gaaah!
Shirone focused harder.
Infinite mental energy flowed through the Spirit Zone, channeled into the crystal orb through his arms.
Neid cursed.
Efficiency: 36%!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: More! I need more power!
The Spirit Zone trembled.
Even its sturdy defenses couldn't withstand the torrent of energy.
It felt like hundreds of compressed forces were about to explode.
Gaaaaah!
Efficiency: 59%.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shirone! Just a little more! Hang in there!
Even the usually calm Iruki shouted in desperation.
The gauge needle swayed wildly like a ship in a storm.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll transform the Spirit Zone into a defensive form.
The frame creaked as it tightened, and a hallucinatory sound of grinding filled his ears.
The moment the pressure converted into output, a sense of deprivation washed over him—as if the world had vanished.
Efficiency: 87%.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh…!
His entire body trembled, his mind shaking violently.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll channel all my energy through the only open path—my palms.
Even with the exit fully expanded, the photon intensity didn't diminish.
Efficiency: 100%.
Gaaaaah!
As Shirone poured out his last ounce of strength, the core lamp turned red.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: It's done, it's done! Iruki, start it!
Iruki, who had been watching in awe, fumbled with the remote control.
His trembling fingers pressed the button, and light shot up through the cables, spreading like a spiderweb across the school.
Dong. Dong. Dong.
The midnight bell rang.
